title: Podman Machine Init cannot create keys windows 10
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have predownloaded the fedora tar file (rootfs.tar.xz) and run "podman machine init --image-path rootfs.tar.xz" to build the default podman machine. Podman 4.3.0 & 4.3.1

When creating a new podman default machine on windows 10 I get the following error:
Configuring system...
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Saving key "podman-machine-default" failed: Bad address
Error: could not create ssh keys: exit status 1


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.Downloaded rootfs.tar.xz (fedora image)

2. Run "podman machine init --image-path rootfs.tar.xz

**Describe the results you received:**
When creating a new podman default machine on windows 10 I get the following error:
Configuring system...
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Saving key "podman-machine-default" failed: Bad address
Error: could not create ssh keys: exit status 1

**Describe the results you expected:**

podman-machine-default to start

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
This worked on podman 4.2.1...

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
"cannot connect to podman"
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
Host:
  Arch: amd64
  CurrentMachine: ""
  DefaultMachine: ""
  EventsDir: C:\Users\user\.local\share\containers\podman\podman
  MachineConfigDir:  C:\Users\user\.config\containers\podman\machine\wsl
  MachineImageDir: C:\Users\user\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl
  MachineState: ""
  NumberOfMachines: 0
  OS: windows
  VMType: wsl
Version:
  APIVersion: 4.3.1
  Built: 1668089925
  BuiltTime: Thu Nov 10 09:18:45 2022
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.18.5
  Os: windows
  OsArch: windows/amd64
  Version: 4.3.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
podman-4.3.1-setup.exe
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
local windows machine

comments.body: Did you start the machine via `podman machine start`?
If you want `podman machine init` to start the machine you can specify the `--now` flag.

I'm sorry for some reason my error message wasn't showing up in my initial comment, but when I clicked the edit button it was there. I fixed the initial post. 

For clarification I get an error when creating the podman machine:
Configuring system...
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Saving key "podman-machine-default" failed: Bad address
Error: could not create ssh keys: exit status 1

The machine never completes the creation process, so I cannot manually start the machine.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
If you do a `podman machine init` without specifying the image path, does the init still fail?

Hi @ashley-cui yes, it happens even with just "podman machine init"

It just downloads the linux image and then fails when trying to create the SSH key.

I was using image-path because I want to attempt to install this in a 100% offline environment at some point.

@Bshukrallah22 any chance that podman --log-level=debug rest_of_command shows anything interesting?  do you suspect any differences in your windows filesystem, like non-default storage locations or some such?

Hi @baude 

I ran the command with debug logging. I got this:

"Running command: wsl cm [-u root -d podman-machine-default ssh-keygen -N -t ed25519 -f podman-machine-default] in dir: C:\\Users\\UserName\\.ssh"
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Saving key "podman-machine-default" failed: Bad address
Error: could not create ssh keys: exit status 1

I've tried elevated and non-elevated  command prompt and powershell.

I'm not sure about non-default storage locations.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@Bshukrallah22 where are you downloading rootfs from?

They should be downloaded/derived from https://github.com/containers/podman-wsl-fedora/releases
Stock fedora images will not work correctly.

If you do not specify an --image-path, and let it download does it work correctly?

Not, OP, but I am having the same errors. Here's my debug log if it helps:

```
PS C:\Users\1114337> podman --log-level=debug machine init
time="2023-02-26T09:13:33-07:00" level=info msg="C:\\Program Files\\RedHat\\Podman\\podman.exe filtering at log level debug"
Extracting compressed file
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
time="2023-02-26T09:13:52-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [--import podman-machine-default C:\\Users\\1114337\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\wsldist\\podman-machine-default C:\\Users\\1114337\\.local\\share\\containers\\podman\\machine\\wsl\\podman-machine-default_fedora-podman-amd64-v36.0.133.tar --version 2]"
time="2023-02-26T09:13:58-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default rpm -q --restore shadow-utils 2>/dev/null]"
Configuring system...
time="2023-02-26T09:14:01-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c grep -q Port\\ 57610 /etc/ssh/sshd_config || echo Port 57610 >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:01-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:02-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat >> /etc/sudoers]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:02-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /etc/systemd/system/systemd-sysusers.service.d/override.conf]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:03-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /home/user/.config/systemd/user/linger-example.service]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:03-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c mkdir -p /var/lib/systemd/linger; touch /var/lib/systemd/linger/user]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:04-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:04-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /etc/containers/containers.conf]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:05-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /etc/containers/registries.conf.d/999-podman-machine.conf]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:05-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c echo wsl > /etc/containers/podman-machine]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:06-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /etc/wsl.conf]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:07-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /usr/local/bin/enterns; chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/enterns]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:07-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /etc/profile.d/enterns.sh]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:08-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /etc/wslmotd]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:08-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /root/bootstrap; chmod 755 /root/bootstrap]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:09-07:00" level=debug msg="Running command: wsl [-u root -d podman-machine-default sh -c cat > /usr/local/bin/proxyinit; chmod 755 /usr/local/bin/proxyinit]"
time="2023-02-26T09:14:10-07:00" level=debug msg="Running wsl cmd [-u root -d podman-machine-default ssh-keygen -N  -t ed25519 -f podman-machine-default] in dir: C:\\Users\\1114337\\.ssh"
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Saving key "podman-machine-default" failed: Bad address
Error: could not create ssh keys: exit status 1
time="2023-02-26T09:14:12-07:00" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
```

When I run the ssh-keygen command, it fails because the `-N` option isn't recognized:

```
PS C:\Users\1114337\.ssh> wsl -u root -d podman-machine-default ssh-keygen -N  -t ed25519 -f podman-machine-default
Too many arguments.
```

Updating the command to exclude `-N` allows the key to generate, but has the bad address as well

```
PS C:\Users\1114337\.ssh> wsl -u root -d podman-machine-default ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f podman-machine-default
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Enter passphrase (empty for no passphrase):
Enter same passphrase again:
Saving key "podman-machine-default" failed: Bad address
```

Like OP, I am unsure about non-standard storage locations.  I am working on a corporate asset, so its possible they have set something up that I am unware of, but I haven't noticed anything like that to date.  Before this issue, the only real problem I ran into was cloning through a proxy. Given the issue comes up even running `wsl` commands directly, I'm wondering if its an issue with `wsl` configuration rather that podman itself.
Yeah I don't believe this is an issue anymore. Feel free to reopen if not.
